title: The AES-256 sync key is deterministically derived from a MetaMask signature so any device rehydrates the same key
content:
All sync data is encrypted with AES-256-GCM in the browser before it leaves the machine; the Bee node never sees plaintext. The encryption key is derived deterministically from the user's wallet so the same user on any device recovers the same key without a server.

Derivation steps:

1. User connects MetaMask.
2. User signs a domain-specific message via `personal_sign`.
3. `keccak256(signature)` produces a 32-byte secp256k1 private key.
4. `SHA-256(private_key)` produces the 32-byte AES-256 encryption key.

The ciphertext format is `[SWE prefix (3 bytes)] [IV (12 bytes)] [AES-256-GCM ciphertext + auth tag]`. The 3-byte prefix `0x535745` marks encrypted payloads; older unencrypted data is detected by its absence, which keeps the reader backward compatible.

Because the key is deterministic from the wallet signature, recovery on a new device only needs the wallet — no cloud key store.
description: personal_sign over a domain message yields a signature; keccak256 of it gives a secp256k1 key; SHA-256 of that gives the AES-256 key — same wallet, same key, no server.
